ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 220, PLAINTIFF FRANK RICCO'S MOTION FOR ORDER TO (1) VACATE DECEMBER 9, 2009 ORDER TO STAY OR DISMISS MATTER ON GROUNDS OF FORUM NON CONVENIENS, AND (2) REINSTATE THE CALIFORNIA MATTER TO THE CIVIL ACTIVE DOCKET. NO APPEARANCE AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO REFILLING THE MOTION IF THE NOTICE OF MOTION STATES THAT PLAINTIFF SEEKS TO LIFT THE STAY RATHER THAN VACATE THE STAY ORDER. PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND INITIAL MEMORANDUM STATE THAT HE SEEKS TO VACATE JUDGE ALVARADO'S ORDER. VACATING AN ORDER IS QUALITATIVELY DIFFERENT FROM LIFTING A STAY. WHILE PLAINTIFF'S REPLY MEMORANDUM STATES THAT HE WISHES TO LIFT THE STAY, THE RELIEF SOUGHT IN THE NOTICE OF MOTION CONTROLS. (SEE CRC 3.1110(A)). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Off Calendar Aug-02-2010 Text Ruling

First Amended Notice Of Motion To Show Cause Re Dismissal On Grounds Of Forum Non-Conveniens
Off Calendar Aug-03-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Eric J. Knapp, Esq. of CARROLL, BURDICK & McDONOUGH 415-989-5900, appearing for defendants / moving party VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.
Michelle M. Cammarata, Esq. of of FILICE BROWN EASSA & McLEOD LLP (510-444-3131), appearing for defendant / joinder Oakfabco, Inc.
Stephen J. Healy, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff Frank Ricco

RULING:
DEFENDANT VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.'s Motion To Show Cause Re Dismissal On Grounds Of Forum Non-Conveniens. Plaintiff did not oppose to the tentative ruling. The Court adopted its tentative ruling: On the Court's order to show cause why this case should not be dismissed, Defendant Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.'s motion seeking dismissal on Grounds of Forum Non-Conveniens and all joinders thereto, the case is dismissed. While plaintiff may have the unqualified right to dismiss his federal court case, his decision to file both the federal court case and this lawsuit shows that he is unconcerned about two parallel lawsuits proceeding at the same time. Since Judge Alvarado has already determined that this court is a seriously inconvenient forum and the four defendants that appear not to be amenable to jurisdiction in Nevada can be sued in a federal lawsuit venued in the Northern District of California, there are alternate suitable fora for all defendants named in this case. Plaintiff's concerns about the manner in which the federal court system has handled asbestos cases in the past, even if true and pertinent to the present, are not a basis to retain this lawsuit in San Francisco Superior Court. Prevailing party is to prepare an order. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To: (1) Lift December 9, 2009, Order To Stay Or Dismiss Matter On Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens; And (2) Reinstate This California Matter To The Civil Active Docket
Off Calendar Jun-29-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
James P. Nevin, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / moving party Frank Ricco
Richard R. Ames, Esq. of CARROLL, BURDICK & McDONOUGH 415-989-5900, appearing for defendant / opposing party VOLKSWAGEN GROUP OF AMERICA, INC.

RULING:
Plaintiff Frank Ricco's motion to lift the December 9, 2009 stay. Argued and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Plaintiff's motion is denied. Assuming without deciding that plaintiff was not required to make any further efforts to establish jurisdiction in Nevada over University Mechanical & Engineering Contractors, Douglass Insulation Company and Advance Mechanical Contractors, a review of the papers filed on this motion shows that these defendants could be sued in federal court as part of plaintiff's federal court action. Because of plaintiff's decision to sue in both federal court and state court, there will be two separate lawsuits by plaintiff pending in two different courts regardless of the decision on this motion: one in federal court and one in Nevada state court or California state court. Given that there is much greater connection between this action and Nevada, forum nonconveniens principles strongly support that the state court action remain in Nevada. Because it appears that University, Douglass and Advance can be sued in the federal court action without destroying diversity jurisdiction over them, this court will set a hearing on its own motion on August 3, 2010 at 9:30am in department 220 why this action should not be dismissed due to the suitability of the two alternate forums of the federal court and the Nevada state court. Defendant Volkswagon is deemed the moving party, briefing schedule to be governed by CCP Section 1005. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)

MOTION For Order To: (1) Vacate December 9, 2009, Order To Stay Or Dismiss Matter On Grounds Of Forum Non Conveniens; And (2) Reinstate This California Matter To The Civil Active Docket
Off Calendar May-18-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff FRANK RICCO's motion for order to (1) vacate December 9, 2009 order to stay or dismiss matter on grounds of forum non conveniens, and (2) reinstate the California matter to the civil active docket is denied without prejudice to refilling the motion if the notice of motion states that plaintiff seeks to lift the stay rather than vacate the stay order. Plaintiff's notice of motion and initial memorandum state that he seeks to vacate Judge Alvarado's order. Vacating an order is qualitatively different from lifting a stay. While plaintiff's reply memorandum states that he wishes to lift the stay, the relief sought in the notice of motion controls. (See CRC 3.1110(a)). Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =(220/HEK)
